rows.value: *I thrive on learning different technologies and enjoy owning projects end-to-end!*

I started writing code professionally in 2008 and have since worked across the *full stack*, building *web and iOS* applications, internal tools, and *cloud* services. Earlier in my career, I took a *lead developer* role, managing a tech team and delivering enterprise projects.

These days, *I use AI* extensively in my work and write mostly JavaScript/TypeScript. Over the years, I have worked in many domains — programming enterprise B2B applications, building AI services & features, implementing multi-region data residency strategies, evolving infrastructure with IaC, ensuring security compliance, and applying CI/CD.

rows.title: Languages
rows.value: Fluent in English, Italian, and Turkish

rows.value: REST APIs • Docker • Git • GitHub • HTML • CSS • Tailwind CSS
I joined Balsamiq, a widely-used wireframing software company, to take over responsibility for various existing custom internal tools and security & compliance initiatives.
My most notable contributions over the years include:

- Building *AI services* via third-party LLM providers
- Architecting and developing custom internal tools (e.g., payment & subscription management via *Stripe*, e-commerce, project management)
- Managing *Elasticsearch* clusters for log management of production apps
- Leading cloud infrastructure modernization with *AWS CDK*
- Performing GDPR compliance audits
- Managing security compliance and bug bounty programs (SOC2/Vanta, Bugcrowd, HackerOne)
- Implementing multi-region data residency for customer data
- Integrating observability and analytics tools (PostHog, Klaviyo)
- Maintaining CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions across 15+ repositories

At WEDO, I had significant opportunities to grow into a lead developer *(full-stack + iOS)* of a mobile and web application team, advise developers from other teams, interact with senior management, and work closely with clients such as *[Bocconi](https://www.unibocconi.it)* and *[CINECA](https://www.cineca.it)*.

During my internship at UniCredit Global Information Services (UGIS), the IT services provider for one of Europe’s largest banks, I visited and analyzed the infrastructure of their data center and prepared a report of my findings.

rows.title: 2005–Present
